content: For a save-system in-memory cache read by main thread and written by async background save tasks: ConcurrentDictionary is thread-safe with lock-free reads and per-bucket write locks, no external lock needed for basic get/set, but GetOrAdd/AddOrUpdate delegates aren't atomic. ReaderWriterLockSlim outperforms plain lock/Monitor in read-heavy, write-rare scenarios (measured ~371ms vs ~981ms in one benchmark) and is the MSFT-recommended pattern for a resource read by many threads and written by one. Recommendation for a save slot cache: ConcurrentDictionary for the key-value store itself (simplest, avoids manual locking), or a single lock/ReaderWriterLockSlim around the whole snapshot+serialize step during SaveAsync to guarantee a consistent point-in-time copy.
